Courses in 2025-2026 Academic Catalog - DRAFT COPY 719

GEOG681 Introduction to Computational Social Science (3 Credits) Introduction to Computational Social Science serves as an introduction to the basis for computational social science (CSS) across diverse disciplines and topics at the intersection of the socio-behavioral sciences, physical sciences, and computing sciences. We will explore the foundations for CSS in different subject domains, as the methods used to build representations of social science entities, phenomena, and processes in computational media. We will also critique applied computational social science as it relates to topics that cut across the socio-behavioral sciences, or that specialize within particular socio- behavioral science domains.

Credit Only Granted for. GEOG681 or GEOG788W.
